title: Ukrainian Navy strikes Russian patrol craft near Kerch Strait
summary: Aurora Intel reports Ukrainian naval forces conducted overnight strikes targeting Russian ship and boat units near the approaches to the Kerch Bridge, hitting the FSB patrol vessel Sobol and the anti-sabotage boat Grachonok and inflicting irreversible and sanitary losses.
contents:
# Ukrainian Navy strikes Russian patrol craft near Kerch Strait

## Strike Confirmation
Aurora Intel synthesises Ukrainian Navy and open-source reporting indicating that Ukrainian forces executed overnight strikes targeting Russian ship and boat units operating near the approaches to the Kerch Bridge, the strategic crossing connecting Russia to occupied Crimea. The Russian FSB patrol boat Sobol sustained hits along with the anti-sabotage boat Grachonok, with Ukrainian sources claiming irreversible and sanitary (killed-in-action) losses against the crews.

## Tactical Significance
The action continues the pattern of Ukrainian USV and missile employment that has progressively degraded Russian Black Sea Fleet capacity, recently described by UK First Sea Lord Admiral Sir Gwyn Jenkins as accounting for roughly one-third of the fleet. Targeting auxiliary patrol craft near Kerch suggests a focus on Russian rear-area defensive screens and counter-USV pickets that protect the bridge.

## Cross-Source Verification
Aurora Intel cross-referenced Ukrainian official statements, satellite imagery hints and Russian milblogger acknowledgments, classifying confidence as medium-high pending visual confirmation of vessel damage. The action lines up with previous strikes on Russian patrol craft and reinforces the strategic vulnerability of the Kerch crossing.
